raw_consumer_statement: On XXXX XX/XX/year>XXXX  I purchased a tree from XXXX XXXX, a local outdoor store. The company had a policy that if the tree dies within a year they provide a full refund. The tree did not live through winter and notified store of this. I no longer had the receipt so called corporate and they guaranteed refund and then went cold talking to me. I then filed complaint with Corporate, no response. I then filed a complaint with Citibank to pull this transaction back. If took them 3 months to figure this out and after even providing info on from XXXX XXXX on their refund policy and proof they promised refund they would not cover this expense or find in my favor. They simply said they, XXXX XXXX, showed proof purchase was made and that's it doesn't matter if tree died. Also said Truth in Lending only allows 60 days to file a complaint. I told them based on my research that Truth in Lending only applies to durable items on complaint timeline not items like this that are not durable. So coming to you as they denied my request for a refund and I do believe this is a obligation the owe me as my credit card company to protect me from fraud and this was straight up fraud from a company that isn't living by their promise for refund.
